function_explanation: Performs weighted back-projection reconstruction of a 3D volume from 2D projection images and their corresponding rotation matrices.
function_docstring:
Performs weighted back-projection reconstruction of a 3D volume from 2D projection images.

This function reconstructs a 3D volume by back-projecting each 2D image into 3D space
(by repeating along the z-axis), applying the inverse rotation transformation, and
averaging all back-projected volumes.

Args:
    images: A numpy array of shape (N, L, L) containing N 2D projection images,
        where each image has dimensions L x L.
    rotations: A numpy array of shape (N, 3, 3) containing N rotation matrices.
        Each 3x3 matrix represents the rotation applied to the original 3D volume
        to produce the corresponding projection image.
    L: An integer specifying the size of the reconstructed 3D volume. The output
        will have shape (L, L, L).

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: A 3D array of shape (L, L, L) with dtype float64 containing
        the reconstructed volume. The reconstruction is the average of all
        back-projected and inverse-rotated 3D images.
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Create sample projection images and rotation matrices
N = 10  # Number of projections
L = 64  # Volume size

# Random projection images
images = np.random.rand(N, L, L)

# Random rotation matrices (should be proper rotation matrices in practice)
rotations = np.array([np.eye(3) for _ in range(N)])

# Reconstruct the 3D volume
reconstructed_volume = reconstruct_back_projection(images, rotations, L)
print(f"Reconstructed volume shape: {reconstructed_volume.shape}")  # (64, 64, 64)
